text: Who discovered the sea route to India?
explanation: Vasco da Gama discovered the sea route to India in 1498.

text: The battle of Plassey took place in?
explanation: The battle established British rule in India in 1757.

text: Who is known as the Iron Man of India?
explanation: Sardar Patel is known for integrating princely states.

text: When did the Jallianwala Bagh incident occur?
explanation: It occurred on April 13, 1919.

text: Which soil is best suited for cotton cultivation?
explanation: Black soil (Regur soil) is ideal for cotton.

text: Who was the first President of Independent India?
explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad served as the first president.

text: The Silicon Valley of India is?
explanation: Bengaluru is the IT hub of India.

text: When was the Quit India Movement launched?
explanation: Launched in August 1942.

text: Who wrote 'Hind Swaraj'?
explanation: Mahatma Gandhi wrote it in 1909.

text: French Revolution started in?
explanation: It broke out in 1789.

text: Which of the following is a Kharif crop?
explanation: Rice is grown during the monsoon season (Kharif).

text: The highest peak in India is?
explanation: Kanchenjunga is the highest peak within Indian borders.

text: Who drafted the Indian Constitution?
explanation: Dr. Ambedkar was the chairman of the Drafting Committee.

text: What is the minimum age to vote in India?
explanation: The 61st Amendment reduced the age from 21 to 18.

text: Which sector contributes most to India's GDP?
explanation: The tertiary (service) sector is the largest contributor.

text: Who was the leader of the Salt March?
explanation: Gandhi led the Dandi March in 1930 against salt tax.

text: The Sun Temple is located at?
explanation: The famous Sun Temple is in Konark, Odisha.

text: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
explanation: Mars has a reddish appearance due to iron oxide.

text: What is the capital of France?
explanation: Paris is the capital and largest city of France.

text: Who was the first woman Prime Minister of India?
explanation: Indira Gandhi served as PM from 1966-77 and 1980-84.

text: The standard meridian of India passes through?
explanation: 82°30'E passes through Mirzapur (UP).

text: Which movement was started after the partition of Bengal?
explanation: The Swadeshi movement began in 1905.

text: The largest producer of wheat in the world is?
explanation: China is the top producer, followed by India.

text: Fundamental Rights are enshrined in which part of the Constitution?
explanation: Part III (Articles 12 to 35) deals with Fundamental Rights.

text: Who said 'Swaraj is my birthright and I shall have it'?
explanation: Bal Gangadhar Tilak gave this famous slogan.

text: Which is the longest river in India?
explanation: The Ganga is the longest river in India (2525 km).

text: The first World War started in?
explanation: WWI began in 1914 and lasted until 1918.

text: Who is known as the Father of the Nation in India?
explanation: Mahatma Gandhi is called the Father of the Nation.

text: The project Tiger was launched in which year?
explanation: Launched in April 1973 to protect tigers.

text: Which constitutional amendment is known as the 'Mini Constitution'?
explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) brought extensive changes.
